reasoning: The Fed officially ended its Quantitative Tightening (QT) program on December 1, 2025, shifting to a 'reserve management' policy under which the balance sheet is expected to grow organically over time to match currency demand. Because the structural reductions have stopped and the current regime favors mild organic expansion, the underlying probability of the balance sheet being higher than the baseline at upcoming weekly readings is greater than 50%. Based on recent low weekly volatility and the consistent organic positive drift, I estimate a 64% chance that the immediate next available period (resolved Feb 8) will print higher. In strict accordance with the system prompt's instruction that uncertainty must increase over time—requiring probabilities for more distant dates to be closer to 0.5—I have compressed the probability for the March 3 resolution date down to 56%.
